string.text: Have you heard about the newly opened places?

Yes, yes, we have already started studying one of them. A few days ago, we sent one of my proteges with a guard to the area known to you as the Promzone, but something went wrong with him, now he refuses to do his job and does not respond. We already have a shortage of people, and allocating them for unexplored territory is simply irrational.\n \nMaybe you would agree to help us? The job shouldn't be particularly difficult, you probably deal with it every day, and my graduate student will reward you generously. We just need you to accompany our worker in the so-called Promzone while he takes some measurements.
Generous, you say? Well, if the work is truly not difficult, then why not.
Maybe next time.

Great, thank you in advance. I will write to him that I am sending another person to help, let's hope he will read it. I will arrange a meeting at the exit of the railway tunnel next to the train, good luck!
Goodbye.
Hello, are you the stalker sent by Sakharov? He's been bothering me with his messages.
Yes, it's me.

Alright, I hope you won't act like the previous one. Sakharov said that there weren't enough people and started hiring anyone for lower priority tasks. One of the "escorts" ended up with me, a stalker just like you, no offense. We went out a few days ago and everything seemed fine, we reached the Freedom base without any incidents and spent the night there. When I woke up in the morning, he was gone, along with almost all of my belongings! I asked around, but no one saw anything or knows anything, as usual around here. The only things he didn't touch were the measurement device and the pistol, so thanks for that. I wrote to Sakharov, and he started assuring me that I can handle it on my own. I got angry and stopped responding to him. I had some money in my pockets to stay at the base for a while.
Sakharov promised that when we finish, you will give a "generous" reward.
I have nothing left except for small change, but I'm sure that when you return to Sakharov, he will easily pay you. He's a man of means, but money is not the most important thing for him.
Oh well, tell me what needs to be done.
Nothing special, I need to take measurements at three points around the factory area, you just cover me.
Sounds really easy, alright, let's go.
Have you finished your measurements?
Yes, I have finished. I will now send everything to Sakharov, inform him about your help, and ask for payment. You can return to him, I still have something to do here nearby.
Okay, bye for now.
Did you receive your measurements from the Promzone?

Yes, I received a message from my assistant and I understand that everything went well? Well, that's good. While you were on your way, the measurements were almost processed. It seems that I was mistaken about their insignificance...
I'm very glad, so what about my reward?

What reward? Ah, yes, one moment. Here, I think this will be enough.\n \nWhile you were here, you showed yourself in a good light, my graduate student praised you very much in his message, although it seemed to me that he was just glad to finish this experiment, anyway, it doesn't matter, maybe you agree to help us in future research of the new area?
Do you need to measure something else at the Promzone?

Not quite, scientific work requires a more careful approach, new research, data comparisons, and much more. After obtaining the initial measurements and realizing the importance of this work, I am now ready to allocate more of my people to it. From you, however, it will be required to perform a slightly different type of work, more, so to speak, closer to you. I promise to provide equally good compensation in the future. Besides, haven't you also become interested?
Not really, but "good compensation" sounds tempting, tell me more.
No, thank you, but I will think about it.

Everyone has their own motives, I understand. Well, I'll try to explain in a few words what we got from the results of the Promzone and what we need from you. As far as I know, your colleagues, that is, stalkers, have already been to almost every corner of this location. Therefore, there are no "brain scorchers" as you call them, or at least they are not active. But our measurements say otherwise, which means that either our data is incorrect, which is unlikely, or there is a ray emission, but of a completely different nature. I have long believed that ray emission can be diverse, but we have had no examples to confirm my hypothesis. If I am right, then we have just come across this very example!\n \nIt's too early to talk about anything yet, we don't have enough data on it for comparison and further analysis. But I think I know where to look. As you know, stalkers come to us from time to time for part-time work, one of them was familiar with our cook even outside the perimeter of the Zone and after a few glasses for the meeting, he told about another stalker who celebrated his birthday in the Bar. He, in a state of alcoholic intoxication, began to complain to everyone about how he found secret documents in one of the basements of the Promzone and how he risked his life for this, but not a single merchant wanted to buy them for the amount he needed. I don't understand why he didn't come to us, although then most likely we wouldn't have considered these documents something important... In general, this is not much and may be just rumors, but at the moment this is the only clue that can bring us closer to understanding this radiation.
Are you indebted to vodka for all your research?

In what sense? Oh, no. Although, to be honest, it applies to a significant portion of them. Let's get back to business. If you want to verify the information, it's best to start with Bar itself. Please try to find out if these documents exist and, if so, locate them. Even if there's a small chance that they contain the information I need, we must try. It could be a milestone in the history of science!
Alright, I understand. I'll try to find out something. Goodbye.
Did anyone here recently celebrate their birthday by any chance?
There was one, I think his name was Hare. Why do you ask?

Well, I'm his friend. We used to wander around the Zone together for a while. He received some important news from the other side, but they can't reach him and asked me to find him. The stalkers said they last saw him here.

Well, I won't be able to help you much with that anyway. He came in seeming cheerful, treated many people, and told me he found some important documents. I skimmed through them a bit while everyone was drinking, compared them to my orders, and told him that his papers were not needed by anyone, but added that I was willing to buy them for a small amount. He started yelling about how difficult it was for him to obtain these documents and that someone was trying to rip him off, so my security guard had to throw him out along with his scribbles.\n \nRumor has it that he left towards the Garbage the next morning. We haven't seen him here since. He probably went to try and sell them to someone else.
Understood, thank you and that's it.

Wait, are you planning to search for him throughout the Zone? It's like looking for a needle in a haystack. How about you do something for me instead, while I ask around my acquaintances? Let's consider it payment for their concern, I promise we'll find your friend, and the task is not difficult.
Not difficult you say? Alright, tell me what needs to be done.

Just without unnecessary questions, okay? We're running low on alcohol, and the next delivery won't be for another week. I managed to arrange with someone to sell me a crate, but these people can't come to the Bar. Everything has already been paid for, you just need to meet them at the Truck Cemetery, pick up the goods, and bring them to me. I'll tell you upfront, the crate is heavy.
Sounds a bit shady, but alright.
Come for a box of vodka?
Yes.
And where is the money?
What money?
What do you mean "what money"? Did you mistake me for a sucker or are you expecting some charity here?
The bartender said it's already paid for. Maybe you should check with your people?
Hold on, just stay put and don't move, or I'll get nervous, got it?\n \n Hmm, yeah, the boss said it's all covered. Alright, here's the box, you can leave while I'm feeling generous.
[Leave]
Here's your crate of drinks, heavy.
Didn't strain yourself? Thanks. Take one bottle, drink with a friend, we just found him. I asked around and people say they saw your Hare in the Cordon, he's sitting in the rookie village.
Alright, thanks.
Hey, is that you, Hare?
What about the documents?
Suppose I am, who are you?
I'm here about the documents you're trying to get everyone to submit.
And why do you need them? You don't look like a trader or an egghead. Well, okay, give me a million and they're yours.
Did you drink too much? Didn't the Bartender tell you how much they're really worth?
You can't even imagine where I've been and what I've been through to get them. I lost a friend there and barely made it out myself!
I guess Sidorovich didn't need your documents either? If these two didn't want to take them, then the rest won't need them either.

How would you know? The Clear Sky guys will take it, they sometimes deal with scientific nonsense and communicate with scientists. As a last resort, I'll go to Sakharov, he'll definitely pay whatever I say.

I just came from Yantar, exactly on Sakharov's order, and believe me, new territories are "of the lowest priority" for them right now. Or do you think they hire random stalkers for important missions? You can, of course, go around half of the Zone with mutants and anomalies if you want, but it won't bring you any more benefits. I suggest you solve everything right here and not go anywhere, just name a reasonable price.
Hmm, let me think for a second.\n \nLet's say 15... no, 20 thousand.
Even Sakharov wouldn't give 20 thousand for this. Let's do 15, I'll have to pay extra from my own anyway, and also go back.
Alright, 15 thousand.
Here's your money.
I'll be back soon with the money.

I only have copies of a few pages from there with me as an example for the traders. The actual documents are hidden at the Garbage right behind the hangar. There will be a van there, and the documents are under the passenger seat. You still going back that way. There are many ears here, you know, and many people are itching to get their hands on them.
 
I... I guess I lied a little about where I found them, and also about my friend who I lost, heh. Well, I did find them on the outskirts of the Promzone, at a gas station. We were sitting in a bar and one of our buddies from the group shared with us how to get to the Promzone. So in the morning, I decided to explore a bit, you know, new places are interesting, and maybe I'll find something along the way. I passed by the Truck Cemetery, where there's hardly ever any living creatures around. Eventually, I arrived at the gas station in the Promzone, checked out a few cars, rummaged through the glove compartment of one sedan, and found these documents. Then I heard a barking nearby and decided it was time to head back.
 
But I can feel that there is some important information in these documents and they were worth my money.
Not very convincing, but I hope it's true for your own good, I hope the documents will be at the Garbage exactly where you said.
I brought your documents from the Promzone, professor.
Wonderful! I never doubted your talents for a second. And how did it go?
I had to run around, carry some boxes, but overall everything went as usual. Here are the documents... By the way, I had to buy them back from that stalker for my own 20,000.

Ah, I understood you perfectly, just a second. Where did I have them... Here, it's 20000 for the documents and some on top of that for your brilliant work, thank you again for your help.\n \nLet's take a look at these documents... Hmm... Interesting... Most of the damaged pages are probably lost forever, but I think some can still be restored. Give me a couple of hours to study these materials, and maybe I'll find more work for you. You can take a break here for now.
Okay.
So how are the documents? Did you find out anything about this ray emission of yours?

To be honest, we didn't learn much about it. Most of the pages are damaged, and the remaining ones cannot be put together into a coherent picture. It seems to be a diary of a radio technician intern, full of physics problem solutions for undergraduates, many of which are incorrect, as well as road maps: routes, hotels, and so on.
So, was all of this in vain?

Not entirely, there is a small connection. He often mentions people who, in his opinion, were from the special services. They would constantly come to his workplace for checks and meticulously inspect the lower premises, which seemed quite strange to him. I suspect he is referring to the radar station at Promzone.\n \nWhile you were looking for this document, we were not sitting idle either. I sent several groups with advanced devices to make more accurate measurements at all the key points in this area. Almost all the groups were able to place the devices in the designated points and return safely, except for one. We don't know what happened to them and we can't establish contact with them.
And what does this have to do with a student's diary?

The task of this group was precisely to install devices at the radar station. I believed that the greatest surge of this emission would come from the factory, but it turned out that the data obtained from these coordinates say the opposite. I suppose that the station itself is the epicenter, but we need to make sure of this first.
 
Not much time has passed since our research groups returned, and I still need their help in analyzing the data. Perhaps the last group simply encountered some difficulties and will arrive a little later, this happens sometimes. But I would like to know for sure that the device is installed... Yes, that my employees are safe and unharmed too, of course.
 
By finding the documents, you once again proved yourself to be a talented specialist... In a certain field, of course. That's why I would really not like to end our cooperation.
So what is required of me? Just go and see if your device is worth it and look for scientists nearby?

Some basic scientific knowledge is required for this job, but thanks to the data already obtained from other devices, I will now be able to easily adjust the most basic parameters on site, and adjust the rest remotely after installation.
 
I will give you a new device and all you will need to do is install it in the radio room at the very top. Just don't press anything on it, except for this big button when you install it.
 
The device is expensive and I can only trust it to you personally, so please, to avoid any incidents, perform this task alone.
Okay, give me your device.

Here you go. And also, if the device is already there, just come back and I'll still pay you. But if it's not, besides installing yours, try to find the old one and return it to me, I'll pay you extra for that, as well as for my missing employees. And if they show up before you reach the station, I'll contact you.
Good.
Professor, I found the bodies of your scientists at the station.
Impo... ... ...tall the devi... ...
Professor...?
Can you hear me? I've installed the device, check it.
Inte... ... ...rfer... ... ...exit the territory of the sta... ...
Oh, great to see you again. What are you doing here?
I'm still running errands for the scientists, and you?

I thought this time I would go a little further, maybe I'll find some more documents while the area is not fully explored. Hey, do the scientists have any more work by any chance? Yours doesn't seem too dusty, that's just what I like.
No idea, go to Yantar and ask yourself, maybe there will be something.
Alright, after I search here for a b... Wait, who is this?
Where?
Brother Vakhar, it's not him again.
Finish off the infidels and let's keep searching.
Are you awake? Don't get up yet.
You.. who..?
According to the token, my name is Illeon.
T... token...?
How are you feeling?
My whole body hurts...
No wonder it hurts, you looked like a sieve when I dragged you here.
Where is here? And why are you wearing their uniform? Who are you anyway?
We're in a collapsed tunnel, in the east. You've been lying here for almost two days. My name is Illeon... At least, I think so. As for the uniform, I have no idea.
Seems like? Don't know?
You don't look very good, although you were very lucky. Everything heals quickly here and I had some good medicine with me, but it's still better for you to rest some more.
I'm fine, just tell me what the hell was that and who are you?
Most likely they were following you, just like they were following those scientists. And as for who I am... The thing is, I don't remember.
What do you mean you don't remember?

The last thing I remember is walking along the road, somewhere in the middle of this area. And then... it's like I woke up... And everything before that just disappeared from my memory.\n \nIt was about a week ago, before that no memories, just fragments that I can't connect together.
But you remember how to shoot?
Yes, some skills and general abilities remained. My hands seem to remember what to do on their own. The same goes for speech.
Do you know where exactly you are or at least what year it is now?

Approximately... When I see or hear something familiar, I can sometimes remember it. The mutants around, for example, helped me remember where I am almost immediately, otherwise I would have ended up in some anomaly.\n \nBut I don't remember the whole area, only a few locations, and even those not exactly.
And looking at your form, does nothing come to mind?

Just now, it's the uniform of the "Monolith" group, who apparently are looking for me. Nothing more from this. The first time I met them was a few days after... well, waking up.\n \nAt first, I was sitting in the basement of the station where they attacked you. I had supplies with me, so I didn't have to go out much. I thought maybe something would become clear during this time, so I only went out a couple of times to look around.
[continue...]

Then I heard people outside, but I decided not to show myself to them and instead follow them when they leave. I could hear a lot from below through a hole in the floor. They started inspecting the station and kept complaining about some professor, and a few minutes later I heard gunshots and screams.\n \nAfter that, I heard one of the attackers asking the wounded person about how to turn something off. His voice seemed familiar to me, but I couldn't remember. The wounded person just screamed in pain and said he didn't know anything, that he was just a guard. They shot him almost immediately. Then the attackers mentioned a couple of phrases, mentioned the name Illeon, and I realized that besides everything else, they are also looking for me.
What happened next?

Nothing... The group that killed those people left. I decided that they couldn't have appeared a few minutes after the arrival of the first ones and most likely were following them or lying in ambush. So I waited until night and decided to look for a more inconspicuous place. This one turned out to be just right.
Then why did you go back there when you saved me? By the way, thanks for that, I never got a chance to thank you.

I was following the stalker, but not to rob or kill him, I'm not a thief or a maniac like those from the station. I wanted to observe... I don't know, what kind of person he is, if he can be trusted. It's just a pity that I didn't have time to save him, everything happened too quickly...
And why do you need some stalker?

Do you know why I'm still here? I have no idea who can be trusted and who can't. Where to go and how to go. I don't even remember all these factions of yours, so you approach someone and when you realize who it is, it's already too late. I need a guide.
A guide? Where exactly?
Seeing the body of one of those people, I vaguely remembered that there are scientists here somewhere. If anyone can help me, I think it's only them.
And that's why you saved me?
Not only because of that... So, will you help?
I think taking you to Yantar is the least I can do in exchange for saving your life.

Thank you. After seeing what those people did to you and those scientists, I thought it would be best not to wear their group's uniform. That's why I took the suit off your acquaintance. I wouldn't normally rob dead people, but I didn't have any other choice.
It's normal here. A dead person won't need it anymore, and in your uniform, we won't be able to walk past other stalkers for even a hundred meters.
I'll change clothes, bring your things, and we can go.
Okay.
Here are your things. By the way, did those Monolith guys say anything?
I don't think so... Although, they called their leader Vakhar.

Vakhar...
 
Remembering the voice I heard at the station, along with his name, something starts to come up in my memory. The "Monolith" has intelligence, sometimes they send small groups deep into the Zone, Vakhar is the head of their intelligence.
 
The mindset of high-ranking soldiers in the faction is slightly different from regular foot soldiers. They don't blindly run into bullets, they calculate their chances and know when to retreat. At first, I wanted to catch up with him and end it all, but then I saw that you were still alive. Besides, others will come to take his place anyway...

It's quite strange to know such details about these fanatics. I would say that you are one of them, but you seem rational, and there are definitely no rational ones among them. I still don't understand anything.
Just like me, that's why we need to reach your scientists.
Alright, let's go.
There is someone here who wants to talk to you.

Ah, there you are. I was starting to find it strange that you haven't been in touch for so long and haven't returned for payment. Judging by your appearance, it seems like you've encountered some difficulties?

Difficulties? They almost killed me there! I barely had time to leave the station and got caught in a hail of gunfire from the Monolith who appeared from around the corner. It seems like they also wiped out your scientists before that. I don't feel like going back there anymore.

That's really unfortunate to hear...
 
Hmm, you say it was the "Monolith" group? It's curious what their interests could be in this area. As far as I know, the northern passage is completely blocked by anomalous fields, which means they must have passed through the Red Forest and the Army Warehouses.
 
Thank you for the information. From now on, I'll send combined groups of the most experienced hired workers there... Wait, so they just wounded you and left?
No, I was saved by this stalker here. He took out most of their squad and then I woke up with patched up wounds in a nearby tunnel. He says he needs to see you because he doesn't remember anything.
But we're physicists, not neurologists, and we already have plenty of work as it is. It's better to consult someone else for cases of amnesia.
When he met me, he was wearing a Monolith suit. And on the way, he remembered details that an ordinary stalker wouldn't know. Just talk to him.
Alright, as a sign of your merits to the scientific community, I'll make an exception this time.
Thank you.

By the way, if you're interested, the device you installed is working properly. Now we're receiving data on ray emission from all points. The radar, as expected, shows the highest spikes of emission. We just need to carefully study the data and maybe do a more thorough check of the station, but this time my most experienced employees will take care of that.
I don't want to know anything more about this station.

I understand, well then, here's your reward for installing the device. As promised, with an additional payment for my employees who were found, even though they died, but now we know what happened to them. And I assume you didn't find their device? Well, never mind... I'll also compensate you for the damage you suffered during the mission.\n \nI'll finish up with something quickly and then examine your comrade. It wouldn't hurt for you to rest as well. If you're interested in the results, come see me in a day.
Alright.
I have installed your device.
No, I was saved by a stalker. He shot most of their squad, and then I woke up with patched wounds in a nearby tunnel. He said he needed to meet with you, but he was killed on the way.

Also killed? Too bad, maybe he could shed some light on the situation...\n \nBy the way, if you're interested, the device you installed is working fine. Now we are receiving data on radiation from all points. The radar, as expected, shows the highest radiation spikes. We just need to carefully study the data and possibly thoroughly check the station itself, but this time my most experienced employees will take care of it.

I understand, well then here is your reward for installing the device. As promised, with an additional payment for my lost employees, even though they died, but now we know what happened to them. And I assume you didn't find their device? Well, never mind... I will also compensate you for the damage you suffered during the mission.\n \nI think our cooperation has come to an end, thank you once again for your help.
How is that stalker doing? Has he regained his memory?

Partially, we took some tests, conducted a small examination, and did a few tests. It turned out to be a truly unique case. This person couldn't remember a single detail about life beyond the Zone perimeter, but when we mentioned almost all artifacts, he was able to list all their known properties!
 
We found it quite strange that he was able to remember the specifics not only of most of the factions surrounding us, but also of the "Monolith" faction in particular, as you mentioned. I believe his information will also help us in our current research.
 
It's still too early to draw any conclusions, a series of additional tests is needed, and I have just ordered the necessary equipment for them.
So should I come back later?
I thought you said you had enough of it last time?
Well, I'm definitely not going to run around the Promzone with your devices while there are crazy fanatics roaming around. Honestly, I'm just curious about what all this means.

Scientific curiosity? That's good, but unfortunately, finding answers to these questions will take some time. I don't know exactly how long, it depends on the delivery dates of the equipment, which can vary from time to time.
 
If you're interested, I have sent some data to my superiors and managed to secure a grant for researching this radiation. Our employees are excellent specialists in their field, but when it comes to completing tasks that are closer to regular stalkers, the results are not always successful. That's why I often have to hire other people, and with the help of this grant, I was able to hire someone known to you as Barman, to search for any information related to our research.
 
So, if you're interested, you can join the search. I have no doubt that your help will expedite the whole process. The payment will come from us, so you can expect a corresponding reward.
Okay, I'll go to him.
No, for now that's enough for me, but I'll think about it.
Sakharov said you have a job?
Yes, I do. By the way, did you find your friend?
Found him, his fanatics killed him at the Promzone.

Yeah... That's life in the Zone, it feels like we just saw each other recently, and a week later the person is gone. I've been hearing rumors that monolithians have been seen more often in this area too.
So, what about the job?

You don't seem too upset. Well, life goes on.\n \nSakharov ordered me to gather information about any mention of research or radiation related to the Promzone. He allocated quite a bit of money, so I've already assigned several groups of stalkers. I can find something similar for you too.
Tell me.

So, from what I know, many people have already been to the Promzone, some just to look around, some to search for something in the far corners. I think everything that was lying around has already been taken, because Sakharov said that his people couldn't find much. I hear rumors about some findings, and we are checking them. The remaining places to check are not pleasant.\n \nYou will need to go to the Dark Valley. A source told me that some of the records from the Promzone are located at the bandits' base.
And I just knock and say, "hello, I'm here for the documents"?

Something like that, yes. But I have already made a deal with their leader, just don't spread the word about it. I will let them know what you look like and they will let you in. Then Sultan will simply give you the documents and that's it.\n \nBut there are two conditions. First - you will be alone. And second - you must arrive at the Dark Valley without any weapons, leave knives and grenades as well. If they notice a weapon in your hands or sticking out of your backpack, they will shoot.
How am I supposed to get there without any weapons?

However you want, use your wits. If you don't have any wits, just run.\n \nAnd one more thing, my contact informed me that for a sum of 4000, he is willing to tell us some details that might interest us. Sultan seems unpredictable to me, you can expect anything from him, so it would be good to have a backup plan. But I won't pay myself, it's up to you to decide.
Alright, here's the money.

I'll send it to him now, wait a minute...
 
Okay, he received it. He says that the documents are in Sultan's room on the table, but he is always there. On the first floor of the unfinished building, there is a pile of dry firewood that can be set on fire. The building won't burn down, but there will be a lot of smoke and commotion for some time.
 
That's it, you can get ready.
I'll manage without the help of your informants.
It's your choice, be careful there.
I don't have the money yet, I'll be back later.
I'm leaving then.
I'm here for the documents for the Barman.
Who do we have here? No hello, no please, just "give me the documents". Did you get something wrong?
Barman said that he already arranged everything with you and that I should just come and pick them up.
Just come? Well, let him come here and pick them up himself. Who are you anyway?

Oh, come on, don't be scared, I'm just joking. It's boring here. If you only saw your face right now.\n \nI have your papers here on my desk, yeah, we took them from a couple of suckers recently. Well, listen, there are mutants running wild in the west, you'll have to kill them, then come back and get your documents... If I'm in a good mood.
More jokes? Do I have to go and beat them up with my bare hands?

Do I look like I'm joking? Alright, I'll give you some knife. We agreed that we'll let you pass through, and will talk about your scientific stuff, so here we are talking, while we have mutants multiplying and bothering us.
Didn't the Barman pay you enough? Think about what's more profitable, betraying him now or milking him constantly?

Well, he did give me a good discount... Alright, let's see how it goes.\n \nBut I'm dying of boredom here anyway. Let's make it simpler, have you seen the movie Gladiator? We have a couple of cameras in the basement and we caught some zombies, we'll take your stuff for a while, lock up the basement, give you a knife, and you'll fight against them, hehe. And then we'll watch this movie with popcorn with the whole gang. Once you kill them, I'll give you your stuff and that folder too, my promise as a man.
... Alright, I agree to fight. Let's just get this over with.
That's a talk. Too bad we don't have that gladiator armor, that would be a spectacle.
Lead your zombies already.
Am I a clown in a circus or something? I didn't come here to entertain you.
Hey, don't talk back to me, got it? If you don't want to, then just leave while I'm still in a good mood.
[Leave]
I've killed your zombies.

That was a great show, haha! I bet it turned out a hundred times better than those cops at the Bar. Well done, you entertained everyone, the guys even fell off their chairs.\n \nHere are your documents on the table, you've earned them. Take them and you can leave.
[Leave]
What's with the smoke? Hey, you, stand at the entrance and don't let anyone in, I'll deal with these idiots.
What's going on here, you idiots, fell asleep with a cigarette again?
Sultan, the room is on fire!
Put it out! Make sure it doesn't spread to the storeroom.
This stalker was just here.
Find that rat! Tell everyone to chase him away.
Brought Sakharov's documents.
Knew you would handle it. Did everything go smoothly?
Not very smoothly, but it could have been worse. Sultan doesn't seem like a reliable business partner to me, I must say.

The benefits of such relationships sometimes outweigh the risks.\n \nGive me your folder, I'll give you the payment. Let's see... Yes, it seems to be what Sakharov asked for, here's your reward for the job. I'll send it to them, let them study it there.
Any news from Yantar?

No, it's still too early for news. He asked to first retrieve all possible records. By the way, two more groups of stalkers that I sent on a search mission have returned before you. One of them brought documents similar to yours, but only one person returned from the second group, and with bad news.\n \nAlthough there were newcomers in the second group, the job didn't seem to be particularly difficult. They just needed to meet a Clear Sky member at Agroprom, who would hand them the documents. They were supposed to meet on a hill near the entrance to the Garbage, where there aren't many mutants. So, the member handed them the documents, but as they were descending the hill, a bloodsucker attacked them in broad daylight.
[continue...]

The returning stalker said that the bloodsucker threw him first, and he rolled down the hill. The second friend froze in fear. The third one was less lucky, the creature tore his chest apart with one strike, then knocked him down and dragged him across the road like a sack.\n \nThe stalker said that it took him another two minutes to understand what had happened. When he came to his senses, he panicked and ran away. What happened to the second person is still unknown. In short, the one who was dragged away by the bloodsucker had the documents. I'm ready to bet my bar that he settled in the nearby tunnel and is hunting our young ones who pass by.
[continue...]

It's better to avoid bloodsuckers, who knows how many of them are there. I was planning to organize a more serious squad later, but it will take time. If you want, I can give you this task and Sakharov will get his documents faster. I'll even pay you extra from my own pocket for the deceased guy, the main thing is to kill that creature. So, are you in?
Alright, I'm in.
I'll be waiting.
I'd rather wait, health is more important. There might be a whole nest of these beasts there.
I understand, then you'll have to wait for about three days, including the time Sakharov will spend studying them. You can go straight to Yantar, I'll send everything there.
[Leave]
Here are the documents from Agroprom.
Great, Sakharov will be glad that we found everything we could. That newbie asked if you saw his partner there, who stayed behind?
I saw, both are dead.

That's it... The search turned out to be not as easy as I expected.\n \nGive me your folder, I'll give you a reward. I think Sakharov will be satisfied for now, but if anything else comes up - I'll send it too. By the way, he asked you to come in.
Going back to Yantar then.
Have you studied your documents?
Yes, I managed to study them in general terms. Did the bartender say that you greatly contributed to obtaining them? Thank you for your efforts.
My colleagues will need one more day or less.
In the process of studying, come back in a couple of days.
Sorry, I'm very busy at the moment. Not all the documents have arrived yet, come back in three days.
So what did you find out?

We found out that the radar is an emitter, but it is controlled from another location, it is still unknown from where exactly. The station itself, apparently, served only as a cover, because we didn't find anything inside that could be somehow related to what was described in the documents. And in the documents themselves, it is mentioned only indirectly, just like the research itself.\n \nThanks to the person you brought, we assume that the purpose of this emitter was to suppress the mind control effect. It won't save people who have fallen under the so-called "brain scorcher", the destruction of neural networks is definitely irreversible. But it is quite capable of suppressing implanted programs in an undamaged brain. The neural connections in such a brain are reformatted, but still intact. It seems that the emitter was developed in case the controlled people regain consciousness.
So that stalker after all...

Yes, now I am completely sure - the person you brought was one of the soldiers of the "Monolith" group. Everything indicates that, including numerous tests with new equipment. His identity is gradually being restored, feelings and emotions from the time before being zombified are returning. But remembering is much more difficult, now he can only recall moments after the zombification effect, everything before that is permanently erased from his logical memory. But his mechanical memory is practically untouched.
I think we both knew that, but it was hard for me to make myself believe it. So, what if he remembers how fun it was to shoot stalkers and kills us all here?
Don't worry and trust in science, young man. That won't happen, the emitter was developed by top-class specialists and... Well, mathematically, there is always a small chance.
I don't even know how to take this, as good news or bad...

I suppose the northern passage of the factory leads to the Radar and the "Monolith" group managed to find a way through the anomalous fields. But they didn't take into account the emitter directed at this sector... Or your acquaintance was just the first test subject.\n \nIn any case, for our common good, we need to prevent this group from finding a way to turn off the emitter.
And how do we do that? Setting up a second barrier there only if, but as you can see, the first one doesn't really help.

The documents describe various modes of the system, one of them is dispersing the beam's energy over a larger radius. Currently, everything seems to be set in focusing mode, directed only to the north. Measurements have shown that the beam's energy is too high for its current task. If we distribute the energy around the perimeter, there should still be enough to prevent anyone from "Monolith" from trying to enter the Promzone territory.
And the documents don't say where exactly the center of this system is?

We need more time to thoroughly study all the documents, maybe there will be an answer there, but I doubt it, the project was strictly classified. Under one of the buildings in the Promzone territory, my employees found an iron door similar to those leading to the laboratories, but they couldn't figure out how to open it. Although there is a whole network of underground communications there, and it is still unknown where this door leads.\n \nWe could search for the entrance, but from what I've heard, the area of the factory has already been occupied by numerous "Monolith" squads.
So what do we do next?

We will need help. The bartender said that Duty has been increasingly encountering the Monolith group near their territory as well. It will be in their own interest, I will talk to the bartender and let him know that you will come, and you will try to convince Duty to help us with his help. I apologize for making you go back and forth, but the topic is quite serious and I wouldn't want anyone else to hear about it.
 
By the way, I have conducted all possible tests on the stalker you brought. It seems to me that the more memories of him return from the time when he was a part of the Monolith group, the more guilt he feels, which has returned to him from an even earlier life.
 
I doubt that I will be able to learn any more useful information from him, and he himself asked to help us. If you want, you can take him with you, he should be by the campfire outside. I also gave him one of our new suits.
Okay, I'll try to talk to Voronin.
[Leave]
How are you feeling?
Fine, I guess. Talk to Sakharov first, he will explain everything.
Alright.
How are you feeling?
Fine, I guess. Did the professor tell you everything?
Did you remember anything?
With the professor's help, a lot... And now I wish I didn't remember it. I understand if you don't want my help, but I still want to join. I feel like I have a lot to fix and a lot to answer for.
Alright, we can go together.
I'm sorry, but I don't think I can trust you right now.
Professor Sakharov asked for your help.

Barman has already told me everything. How can we help? We don't have enough people, and with each passing day, there are even fewer of them. Almost every patrol sent to the north or east encounters squads of Monolith soldiers. Several squads have settled in the Promzone, and they constantly patrol the area. I definitely don't have enough people for that, and I won't send them there to certain death.
But this is the only opportunity. If we don't act now, there will be even more of them in the future.
What makes you think that some installation can stop their attacks? According to what Barman told me, all the scientists have are assumptions. They don't even know where this thing is located.

We have already seen how it affects them, and so far, all of Sakharov's assumptions have been correct. For now, the Monolith fighters are infiltrating the Promzone in small squads, but if they manage to disable the emitter and open the northern passage, there will be a whole army instead of just a few squads.
Even if we decided to do it, as I said, I don't have enough people.
We can ask the Freedom faction for help. They need it even more than you do.
Let the fanatics kill them all, I'll be happy about it.
After they kill them, who do you think will be next? You can't even leave the Bar peacefully right now. I suggest only a temporary truce.

We... Let me think...\n \nThey recently attacked our reconnaissance squad at the Warehouses. They killed one of our men, wounded another, and the last one managed to escape to report it. The wounded one is probably taken captive. I'm willing to negotiate with them, but only if they give me back the captured soldier. If you want, you can try to talk with them about it.
Looks like I'll have to.
Have you heard about the Promzone?

What exactly? We don't need to hear anything, we can see it all ourselves. Fanatics are rushing there from every crack, shooting at everyone who moves. They used to go through the Red Forest, but those were mostly small reconnaissance groups attacking the Barrier. There are too many mutants and anomalies there. It's much more convenient for them to go in large squads from the Radar. Now they still come in small groups from the forests, but much more often. We simply don't have enough people to constantly control the entire territory, and I still have more wounded every day. I don't know how many of them are already in the Promzone, but I think there are many.

Sakharov thinks that there is a emitter in the Promzone that prevents them from going there directly from the Radar, although there is a passage known only to the Monolithians. The emitter only affects zombified people, it doesn't seem to have any effect on regular stalkers. If they disable this device, they will be able to move freely to the south in large groups.

An emitter against the fanatics? Like a reverse brain scorcher? That's a useful thing. We just found the plan of the entire factory, including the underground tunnels, when the location was not yet full of these psychos.
Does it say where the entrance is?
It's all here. Entrances and exits, there are a couple of entrances to the bunker, but there is no plan of the bunker itself. It's unclear what's inside. Did you want to suggest something?
Yes, someone needs to infiltrate this bunker and reconfigure the emitter so that it covers the entire territory of the Promzone.
And you and your eggheads know how to do that? Well, thanks for sharing the plans, good luck to you there.

Soon everyone will be too busy to joke if no one does it. I asked the Duty guys, they don't have enough people for that. But they are willing to agree to a temporary truce if you agree to help... And return their captive.
Oh, you're a joker too, I see. Why should we take risks, especially for these idealists? To us, those fanatics are no different from them.

At least you can talk to them. If the monolithians start passing through the Radar in large crowds, do you think they will just go south and forget about you? You will be surrounded, and it will be impossible to negotiate with them anymore, and I guess you don't have enough people for a couple more new barriers.

Damn, I don't know... Well, maybe for a while, but we still won't be able to help until we have a good supply of weapons. The mutants have now blocked the road from the Mercs, and all my people are already busy with patrols.\n \nIf you can wipe out the mutant lair in the Grimwood near the pond, which constantly attacks our people and prevents them from meeting with the hires, then we can talk.
Alright, I'll do it.
Shot the mutants in Grimwood.
Oh, finally some good news for a change. Well, tell me what you wanted.
You will give the Duty their man, then we will meet and discuss how to eliminate the fanatics. After that, we will reconfigure the emitter and everything will go back to normal.

It sounds simple, but I don't know if we can trust them. We don't even know how many crazies have settled in the Promzone, we didn't count the ones running by. But let's meet them anyway and see what happens.\n \nTell them to come to the farmstead, I will send one of my most experienced people to talk to them. We will clear the area so that we don't shoot each other at the slightest noise. I will bring their servant now and you can go.
Okay, I will pass on the message.
Brought your soldier.
Didn't think he was still alive, thanks. What did they say?
They said they are ready to meet and discuss the situation at the farmstead.

Next to their base, so it's easier for us to wipe them out? Fine, my captain is currently patrolling the Wild Territory, I will tell him to go to the Warehouses, but only if you're going to be there too. If anything happens to him, you will be held responsible.
Understood.

So, finally you're here.\n \nSo listen, I've come up with a couple of ideas. Judging by the factory building plan, the entrance to the laboratory is located in the eastern part of it. The fanatics are scattered around the perimeter, but especially in the eastern part. We'll split into two groups, the first one will attack from the main entrance and lure as many Monolith members as possible from the eastern part. The second group will break through the hole in the fence from the east.
Sakharov said that the door cannot be opened, how will we get in there?

This door can only be opened from the inside, but there is another entrance through the bunker at the very bottom, which is located above the laboratory. At the end of the lower corridor, there is a ventilation shaft, the entrance to the bunker should be behind it.
Doesn't sound great, but if there's nothing better...
In my opinion, it's a great plan. Alright, I think that's it, let me know when you're ready.
Alright, we'll write it down.
It might work, I'll pass everything to Voronin. Go to the Barman when you're ready, he'll contact Sakharov.
Understood.
Pass on to Sakharov that Duty and Freedom have agreed to help.
Already contacted, he asked you to pass a small instruction on setting up the emitter.
Wait, me? And what about his scientists?

He mostly has hired guards capable of scaring off bandits and shooting at small mutants. As far as I know, it will be quite hot there, they haven't prepared for assaults on the fortifications. And the scientists themselves either won't go under fire with you, or will run away at the first opportunity.\n \nSakharov said that you are already familiar with the principle of the emitter's operation in general terms. He also asked to convey that it's not difficult there, you just need to pull a couple of levers.
Oh... Alright, tell me what needs to be done.

So, listen. When you find yourself inside and find the emitter itself, there should be two cabins, they are located higher than everything else. First, you will need to pull the lever in both of them. After that, go to the control center, which should be nearby, and on one of the panels there should be the last lever. That's all you need to change the mode. Seems not difficult.
 
Sakharov added that according to their calculations, all the Monolith members in the area should drop dead or have their minds blown by a sudden surge of something. So when you figure out the emitter, you can safely get out.
 
There's one more thing. They also asked to search the laboratory for anything related to the emitter. If you manage to find anything, bring it to Sakharov, he's willing to pay for each item.
If we can even get there at all.
Let me know when you're ready, I'll write to everyone to move out.
Ready to go now, write to everyone.
I'll let you know when I'm ready.
What's going on there?

We just arrived ourselves. We looked around a bit, it seems like they're starting to fortify the area. We'll signal the Duty to attack. As soon as we hear shots, some of the fanatics should run towards them, and that's when we'll start too.
 
Alright, one more time. As soon as we reach the building around the corner, immediately go down the hatch. The main entrance is already barricaded, we'll stay behind to cover. Then go down the corridor to the main staircase, then go to the lowest level and to the ventilation. Once you deal with the emitter, exit through the main door, no need to crawl back into the bunker.
 
I bet the Monolith are wandering around the location, and as soon as they find out about our attack, they'll start converging from all directions. We'll try to hold them off for as long as possible.
Understood.
Oh, did you kill the bloodsuckers at Agroprom?
Yes, and you...

I'm a stalker who was in that group. Barman told me that you found their bodies...\n \nAfter that, I went on a bender. Then I heard conversations in the Bar about how everyone is screwed because of the Monolithians. I asked Barman, he explained briefly, and I begged to join. Maybe now I can do something useful for everyone, Barman even found me an old suit.
It will be dangerous for newcomers here, but any help will be useful.
We're here, that hatch to the right of the building. Go, we'll cover as much as we can.
Kill everyone you see.
The traitor is here too! Kill him first!
Stalker, you did it! Look, the fanatics are all dead or gone crazy.
Seems like we reconfigured the Promzone emitter.

Did you manage to do it after all? That's great news!\n \nYou've done a great service to all people and to us in particular. Here, take this, you've earned it. The bartender told me that if you haven't visited the leaders of the Duty and Freedom factions yet, they are also ready to reward you materially.
Thank you, what's next?
Now we'll have time to study the documents and the emitter itself more carefully, maybe we'll find something interesting related to all this. That's all for now.
Understood. Well then, goodbye.
I was told that you wanted to discuss something about the Promzone?

I heard the fight was tough, I don't even know what you had to deal with inside those bunkers. But I'm glad you made it... Now I hope everything will be as it was before.\n \nI asked Petrenko to pick something up for you, take this.
Thank you.
I heard you wanted to talk about the Promzone?
Dude, we did it! Now the fanatics will keep rushing our fortifications, where we can at least hold them back.\n \nHere, we prepared something for you.
Thank you.
I found some things in the Promzone laboratory.
Alright, let's see... Ah... Oh, this is interesting.\n \nWe can use this, thank you. And here's your money.
Thank you.
You managed to gather enough information.\n \nHere's a special reward for your efforts.
We will need more items with information, bring us everything you could find.
This will be useful, thanks again.
Alright, bye.
Come with me.
